focal_character.name: Nantale Namakula
focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Working at her community pharmacy on Nakasero Road, verifying dispensed prescriptions against patient record cards before release.

problem_structure.unknown: How to fill the order correctly when two candidate solutions both appear to match the written slip
data: Two sets of containers on the shelf, both matching the written order slip — one set matches by standard-formulation protocol, the other matches when cross-referenced against the reference chart
A written order slip on the counter beside a flat square verification panel
A reference chart pinned to the wall above the shelves showing formulary cross-references and interaction corrections
A flat square verification panel on the counter, dark and faintly warm
problem_structure.condition: The filled order must be placed on the verification panel and the panel must light green — a protocol-only fill that ignores the reference chart will not pass

gate_details.correct_approach: Read the reference chart to cross-check the order slip against known interactions and corrections; select the chart-correct set rather than the protocol-correct set; place it on the verification panel; press the panel and wait for the green light before assuming closure.

new_texture: Community pharmacies on Nakasero Road fill overnight prescriptions left by junior dispensers, with patient record cards stored in a file rack behind the counter for cross-reference during morning verification.
Pickup trays hold filled prescriptions awaiting final verification before release to patients.
Regular customers at neighbourhood pharmacies maintain years-long relationships with their pharmacist.

anchor_update.observation:
Grade 2. Specialist approach. Room organised by chemical category — all analgesics grouped, all antibiotics grouped, all cardiovascular agents grouped. Reference chart outside groups, dim. Domain boundary visible as gap between analgesic and cardiovascular clusters. Underlined: gap between groups contained the correct formulation. Solver did not look at the gap. Register 2 at minute 8, lost at minute 11. No closure.

Grade 2. Tester approach. Room mirrored each configuration in sequence — twelve arrangements, each ending at panel. Panel present in every configuration, unactivated. Underlined: panel was the test. Solver never asked what was being tested. Register flicker throughout. No sitting down. No closure.
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor's Kampala notebook page omits the correct approach for the first time, recording only the two failed approaches before departing — suggesting the anchor either knew Nantale would succeed or deliberately left the page as a clue rather than a complete record.

macro_mystery_update.evidence: The Kampala notebook page records two Arc 2 wrong-approach types (specialist, tester) using the anchor's diagnostic framework, expanding the taxonomy across five cities; the guild files the gate report as an 'anomalous closure' with the word 'checking' written unconsciously in the margin, continuing the pattern of the guild noticing but failing to interpret the anchor's accumulating records.
